content.claim: Between 2010 and 2020, the proportion of students identified with special educational needs in Hungarian primary education rose from 6.7% to 7.7%, and in secondary education from 3.9% to 6.2%.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Political feasibility lens
limitations: The literature itself frames prevalence increase as an open interpretive question rather than settled fact about true underlying need
The trend could reflect diagnostic drift, policy incentives, or labeling practice changes rather than genuine increases in disability prevalence
International comparison data on whether this rate is unusual for Central Europe is not provided in the notes
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
